title: Newtonia leucocarpa
taxonomy: Newtonia leucocarpa is classified in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships to other plants within the family. Within the family Rubiaceae, Newtonia leucocarpa is further classified in the genus Newtonia, which is a genus of flowering plants. The genus Newtonia is part of the tribe Vanguerieae. The classification of Newtonia leucocarpa reflects its evolutionary history and its place within the plant kingdom.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Newtonia leucocarpa, commonly known as the White-flowered Newtonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Newtonia leucocarpa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Newtonia leucocarpa is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health ailments, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in some parts of the world.
